chapter_context.summary: Rebukes the elders who carry idols in their hearts while coming to inquire of the prophet, declaring that God will answer them Himself, and asserting that even if Noah, Daniel, and Job were in the land, they would only deliver their own souls.

outline.text: Elders Inquiring with Idols in Their Hearts Rebuked
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elders Inquiring with Idols in Their Hearts Rebuked (14:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Inveitable Four Sore Judgments: The Noah, Daniel, and Job Principle
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Inveitable Four Sore Judgments: The Noah, Daniel, and Job Principle (14:12-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 14, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
